perturbation.original_text: 5.5 Documentation of Costs. Each Participant acknowledges and agrees that ---------------------- in accordance with the terms and conditions of the Cooperative Agreement, NIST will have right of access to sufficient records and information of each Participant to insure full accountability of all funding under such Cooperative Agreement. Each Participant is responsible for assuring that adequate documentation supporting all claims to NIST is retained and is accessible in the event of an audit.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 Documentation of Costs. Each Participant acknowledges that NIST may request access to records and information to ensure accountability of funding. Each Participant will use reasonable efforts to maintain some documentation, if practical, to support claims to NIST in case of a potential audit.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates retaining and providing access to documentation to support claims to NIST. The modified version uses softer language such as 'may request,' 'reasonable efforts,' and 'if practical,' making the obligation to retain and provide documentation ambiguous and discretionary.
perturbation.law_explanation: 2 CFR § 200.302(b)(3) requires recipients of federal funds to maintain records that adequately identify the source and application of funds. The modified text weakens this requirement by making the retention of adequate documentation discretionary, thus contradicting the regulation's mandate for accountability and audibility.

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts 2 CFR § 200.302(b)(3), which requires recipients of federal funds to maintain records adequately identifying the source and application of funds. The original text mandates retaining and providing access to documentation, while the changed text uses 'may request,' 'reasonable efforts,' and 'if practical,' weakening the obligation. The scraped snippets do not provide the exact wording of 2 CFR § 200.302(b)(3), but the law explanation states this regulation mandates accountability. The shift from mandatory to discretionary compliance creates a clear contradiction because the modified text no longer ensures the required level of accountability and audibility.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 Disclosure and Reporting of Subject Inventions. Each Participant shall ---------------------------------------------- disclose, as promptly as practical, to the Project Manager in writing an Subject Inventions of which said Participant becomes aware. The Project Manager shall have responsibility for reporting such Subject Inventions to NIST pursuant to the Cooperative Agreement; however, any follow up reporting (e.g., as to decisions on filing patent applications) shall be the responsibility of the Participant(s) owning that Subject Invention.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 Disclosure and Reporting of Subject Inventions. Each Participant should consider disclosing Subject Inventions to the Project Manager when appropriate. The Project Manager may report such Subject Inventions to NIST; however, any follow-up reporting will be addressed on a case-by-case basis.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the prompt disclosure of Subject Inventions to the Project Manager. The modified version uses softer language like 'should consider' and 'when appropriate,' making the disclosure discretionary. Additionally, changing the follow-up reporting to a 'case-by-case basis' removes the responsibility of the Participant(s) owning the Subject Invention to file follow-up reports.
perturbation.law_explanation: 37 CFR § 401.14(c)(1) requires contractors to disclose each subject invention to the Federal agency within a specified timeframe. This change makes that disclosure voluntary, thus violating federal regulations regarding intellectual property developed with federal funding.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates the prompt disclosure of Subject Inventions, whereas the changed text uses discretionary language ('should consider'). According to the Law Explanation, 37 CFR § 401.14(c)(1) requires contractors to disclose each subject invention to the Federal agency within a specified timeframe. Therefore, by making the disclosure voluntary, the changed contract contradicts the legal requirement for mandatory disclosure, which undermines federal regulations regarding intellectual property developed with federal funding.

perturbation.original_text: 9.8 Insurance. Each Participant shall obtain and maintain appropriate --------- public liability and casualty insurance, or adequate levels of self insurance, to insure against any liability caused by that Participant's activity under this Agreement and/or the Cooperative Agreement. Within ninety [90] days after this Agreement has been signed by all the Participants, each Participant who is not entirely self insured for matters relating to this Agreement shall obtain from its relevant insurers, and deliver to the other Participants, a written waiver of subrogation for the benefit of the other Participants under each and every purchased insurance policy which said Participant has which is relevant to said Participant's activity under this Agreement. Such a written waiver will also be obtained and promptly delivered for each relevant new or renewal policy obtained by either Participant during the term of this Agreement. In lieu of obtaining such a waiver for a particular policy of its purchased insurance, a Participant may instead arrange to have the other Participants named as additional insureds on said policy. Each Participant shall be responsible for its own self insurance retention's and for all deductible amounts under its purchased insurance.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.8 Insurance. Each Participant should consider maintaining some form of --------- public liability and casualty insurance, or self-insurance, to address potential liability. Participants may attempt to obtain a waiver of subrogation within a reasonable timeframe. Alternatively, they might explore the possibility of naming other Participants as additional insureds. Each Participant remains responsible for its own self-insurance retentions and deductible amounts.
perturbation.explanation: The original text creates a clear obligation to obtain and maintain insurance and to secure a waiver of subrogation or name other participants as additional insureds. The modified language uses softer terms like 'should consider' and 'may attempt', making these requirements discretionary and ambiguous, weakening the risk mitigation measures established in the original contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: FAR 52.228-5 Insurance - Work on a Government Installation
perturbation.law_explanation: FAR 52.228-5 requires contractors performing work on government installations to maintain specific insurance coverage. By making the insurance requirement discretionary, the modified text potentially contradicts this federal regulation, especially if the Project involves work on a government installation.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 48 CFR § 52.228-5 - Work on a Government Installation. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 48 CFR § 52.228-5 - Work on a Government Installation. CFR prev | next 52.228-5 Insurance—Work on a Government Installation. As prescribed in 28.310 , insert the following clause: Insurance—Work on a Government Installation (JAN 1997) (a) The Contractor shall , at its own expense, provide and maintain during the entire performance of this contract , at least the kinds and minimum amounts of insurance required in the schedule or elsewhere in the contract . (b) Before commencing work under this contract , the Contractor shall notify the Contracting Officer in writing that the required insurance has been obtained. The policies evidencing required insurance shall contain an endorsement to the effect that any cancellation or any material change adversely affecting the Government's interest shall not be effective (1) for such period as the laws of the State in which this contract is to be performed prescribe or (2) until 30 days after the insurer or the Contractor gives written notice to the Contracting Officer , whichever period is longer. (c) The Contractor shall insert the substance of this clause, including this paragraph (c), in subcontracts under this contract that require work on a Government installation and shall require subcontractors to provide and maintain the insurance required in the Schedule or elsewhere in the contract . The Contractor shall maintain a copy of all subcontractors ' proofs of required insurance , and shall make copies available to the Contracting Officer upon request. (End of clause) [ 54 FR 34758 , Aug. 21, 1989; 62 FR 239 , Jan. 2, 1997] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated insurance coverage, whereas the modified text uses discretionary language ('should consider,' 'may attempt'), weakening the obligation. FAR 52.228-5 explicitly states, 'The Contractor shall... provide and maintain during the entire performance of this contract, at least the kinds and minimum amounts of insurance required,' (Snippet 1 & 2). The change from 'shall' to 'should consider' directly contradicts this mandatory requirement, creating a significant legal contradiction. The original text creates a clear obligation to obtain a waiver of subrogation, while the modified language weakens this obligation.
